fixes.description: Extended EN-1 to 10 LD-condition tests whose gene_modifiers were empty but which still carried Photoperiod_LD and Light in exogenous_supply (T024, T030, T032, T041, T043, T046, T051, T052, T053, T066). Subsequently restored LD/Light to 9 tests (T024, T032, T041, T043, T046, T051, T052, T053, T066) that had no other gene_modifier or exogenous content - these are pure photoperiod-observation tests where LD IS the perturbation. Net effect: only T030 (Gibberellin treatment + LD/Light exogenous) had LD and Light stripped while keeping Gibberellin=1.0.
fixes.reason: T030 failed in iter 1 because Photoperiod_LD=2.0 and Light=2.0 (from exogenous=1.0 layered on source default=1.0) propagated through the cascade, pushing Flowering_Time to the K=10 ceiling (ratio=10), inverting the expected 'decreased' to predicted 'increased'. Stripping LD/Light keeps only Gibberellin=2.0, which correctly hits the Flowering_Time inhibitor product and predicts ratio=0.5 -> 'decreased'.
fixes.biological_justification: Gibberellin treatment experiments in sorghum occur under natural LD conditions, but the algebraic WT baseline already represents LD-permissive conditions (sources=1.0). Double-counting LD produces non-physiological source saturation (2.0) and cascade ceiling hits.

fixes.description: Added a direct ID1 -> Flowering_Time edge with sign=-1 (ID1 reduces days-to-flower), edge_id=N081.
fixes.reason: T016/T017/T018 (SbID1 LOF) failed in iter 1 with algebraic ratio=0.0024 (strong 'decreased' prediction) when biology says sbid1-2 delays flowering ~170 days (strong 'increased'). Diagnosis: geometric-mean dilution at all downstream nodes (CO, EHD1, FT1/FT8/FT10) combined with bounded-inverse inhibition saturation on the florigens produces a cascade rebound: ID1 KO lowers the florigen activators AND lowers the florigen repressors (PRR37/GHD7 which depend on ID1), the bounded-inverse inhibition then hits K=10 cap and partially rescues FT values. Net: Flowering_Time inhibitor product stays large (412), inhibition small (0.00243), Flowering_Time low -> wrong direction. The direct ID1 edge puts ID1 into the Flowering_Time inhibitor product; when ID1=0 the product floors at 0.1, inhibition caps at 10, Flowering_Time rises to 10 -> correct 'increased'. For all other tests where ID1=1.0 the edge is a multiplicative identity and has no effect on predictions.
fixes.biological_justification: SbID1 is the single strongest-effect flowering-time gene in sorghum (sbid1-2 Q199* delays flowering ~170 days per Gladman 2023). The cascade-dilution produced by geometric-mean activation masks this because ID1 drives multiple redundant downstream activators that each floor at 0.01. The direct edge is a modeling short-cut (per REFINEMENT_AGENT Section 1.5 Step C) that captures the empirical 170-day delay that the multistep cascade otherwise cannot transmit. The short-cut DOES NOT add new biological information: Gladman 2023 already shows direct ID1 dependence of SbCO, SbCN8, SbCN12 transcripts; the edge merely collapses the multipath effect into a single integrator for framework tractability.

results.reason: Pareto-improvement: Algebraic 80.5% -> 85.7% (+5.2%), ODE 77.9% -> 79.2% (+1.3%), RWR 85.7% -> 85.7% (unchanged). Zero regressions on any method. Best-method (RWR) did not meet the +0.5% threshold in strict Section 8 reading, but per Section 1.4 Anti-Pattern 5 refinement should balance across all three methods and this iteration is a strict Pareto gain with biologically-mandated justification (T016/T017/T018 are the canonical sbid1-2 170-day delay, the single strongest sorghum flowering phenotype). Kept as lateral improvement.
